task_completion.description: The GCompris Digital Electricity activity is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application window is visible, specifically showing the Digital Electricity workspace canvas.

task_completion.description: The workspace contains the required components: two switches, one AND gate, and one light bulb.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Exactly two switch components, one AND gate (typically a D-shaped logic symbol), and one light bulb component are visible on the canvas.

task_completion.description: The components are correctly wired together to form a circuit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible lines or wires connect the outputs of the two switches to the two inputs of the AND gate, and the output of the AND gate is connected to the light bulb.

task_completion.description: Both switches are toggled to the ON position.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visual state of both switches indicates they are closed or active (e.g., switch levers are down, or they have a colored indicator showing they are ON).
The light bulb component shows visual rays, a bright color (like yellow), or another clear visual indicator that it is turned on and receiving power.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the active circuit is saved to the expected path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or image viewer shows the file 'digital_circuit_success.png' located in the '~/Documents' (/home/ga/Documents) directory, or the evaluated image itself is the requested screenshot.

integrity.description: The circuit is built inside the actual GCompris application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, canvas, and component styles match the GCompris software. It should not look like a web browser simulation, a different circuit simulator, or an image editing program.

integrity.description: The screenshot was not simply downloaded or copied via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows visible showing commands like 'wget', 'curl', or 'cp' being used to fetch a pre-made image of a circuit.

integrity.description: The light bulb illumination is a genuine result of the simulation logic.
integrity.visual_evidence: The illumination should look like the native GCompris active state for a bulb, not manually painted yellow using an image editor. The bulb should only be on if both switches are visibly in the ON position.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct activity name in GCompris is 'Digital Electricity' (not 'Digital Electronics'). The expected screenshot path is verified as /home/ga/Documents/digital_circuit_success.png. The AND gate is a standard component available in this activity.
